workstream_catalog.title: Odylith Product Self-Governance and Consumer Hard Cut

why.problem: Odylith's runtime code already lives in the public repo, but the public repo still does not own the product's component registry, product component specs, or local governance records. That leaves a downstream consumer repo acting like the product system of record.
why.customer: - Primary: maintainers shipping Odylith as a real installable product. - Secondary: consumer repos that should adopt Odylith without inheriting its internal product-governance authorship.
why.proposed_solution: - add Odylith-owned product governance truth to the public repo; - publish Odylith-owned component specs and registry data from the public repo; - keep consumer-repo truth local to each consumer; - repoint downstream consumers to installed Odylith docs and CLI surfaces instead of consumer-local product records.
why.opportunity: By letting the public repo govern itself, Odylith can ship a cleaner product boundary, cleaner public docs, and a clearer install contract for consumers.
why.why_now: The runtime extraction is already far enough along that the missing piece is authority, not scaffolding. This is the point where Odylith becomes the source for itself.
why.founder_pov: Odylith should not depend on a consumer repo to explain what Odylith is. The product needs to own its own vocabulary, specs, surfaces, and evidence if it is going to be credible as a standalone system.

next_tasks: Source/runtime truth is still duplicated by stale generated outputs, tests, and duplicate root `docs/` copies that point back at deleted shared-doc governance paths.
A downstream consumer repo still carries Odylith product docs or registry truth as local repo truth instead of consuming installed Odylith surface/runtime paths.
The public repo has its own `odylith/registry/source/component_registry.v1.json`.
The public repo keeps Odylith-owned component specs inside the owning `odylith/` surface/runtime folders and does not route governance truth back through shared repo-level doc buckets.
The public repo has its own `odylith/radar/source/`, `odylith/technical-plans/`, `odylith/casebook/bugs/`, `odylith/atlas/source/`, and `odylith/compass/runtime/`.
Consumer product-component references resolve to installed `odylith/...` surface and runtime docs.
